summary: Read the actual traceback before guessing the cause of an error
problem: First instinct is to guess what went wrong and start making changes, which wastes turns on the wrong fix
solution: Scroll to the bottom of the traceback first - the root exception is almost always the last 3-5 lines, not the middle of the stack
root_cause: Long tracebacks make the real error visually hard to find; the top of the stack is where execution started, not where it broke
